sample_tweets.author: Jimfrombaseball
sample_tweets.text: "Whoever wants to know the heart and mind of America had better learn baseball, the rules and realities of the game, and do it by watching first some high school or small-town teams. The big league games are too fast for the beginner and the newspapers don’t help. That baseball fitly expresses the powers of the nation’s mind and body is a merit separate from the glory of being the most active, agile, varied, articulate, and brainy of all group games. Baseball takes its mystic nine and scatters them wide. A kind of individualism thereby returns, but it is limited—eternal vigilance is the price of victory. Just because they’re far apart, the outfield can’t dream or play she-loves-me-not with daisies. For those whom civilized play doesn’t fully satisfy, there will be provided a scapegoat in a blue suit, the umpire, yell-proof and even-handed as justice, which he demonstrates with outstretched arms when calling “Safe!” "Baseball: A Literary Anthology." Jacques Barzun. "Friday Night Date" John Falter, 1958. "In baseball as in love, you have to be willing to adapt and change your strategy when things aren’t going well."

sample_tweets.author: legaltweetz
sample_tweets.text: That’s because of the nonbinary communists and spicy straight “queers.” Things went way too far. I remember how jubilant I was when gay marriage was legalized. I felt like that was justice. Today, I feel like the movement is about fetish and trans. I have a lot to say about this given my incredibly long history in the LGBT community. I was behind the curtain. Here’s what happened. It’s become incredibly Orwellian. Ask me how I know. I’m the ultimate apostate to the TQ+ cult. Given my experience within LGBT activism, I can tell you exactly what happened: mission creep. Once gay marriage was won, all the LGBT orgs wanted to keep the money and jobs flowing. So they shifted gears into trans to keep the money flowing. And flow it did. It wasn’t immediate. It was actually slow and insidious. I remember, in 2015, a few months before Obergefell was decided, working with some of the orgs to combat all the bathroom bills cropping up. The bathroom bill issue back then was thrust upon us as activists. However, conservatives were way ahead of their time on the bathroom bills. Their problem was messaging. At the time, it just sounded weird and invasive to normal people. Little did we know the conservatives were onto something. Then my own life intervened. I became very sick and disabled and stopped working in 2017. I still did grassroots volunteer-type stuff for LGBT causes, but I stopped working with orgs because I was incapacitated. I was very out of the loop on what orgs were doing during that time since, I mean, I didn’t leave the house. I came back to the LGBT orgs around 2022 as I slowly started reemerging from my illness into a more functional state. By the time I came back in ’22, it was all trans, all the time, everywhere. I was trans-identified at the time. However, when I started transitioning in 2018, I did so very privately. I didn’t have any friends because of my disability (turns out you also lose friends because of a major illness 🤷‍♂️). I was totally anonymous online for the first years of my transition. I was quiet, private, and definitely NOT a TRA whatsoever. Anyone who remembers 2022 remembers peak woke and peak trans. It was comply or be canceled. Even as a trans-identified female back then, I was privately taken aback by what activism had morphed into during my absence from the orgs themselves. The orgs totally erased the LGB from advocacy. I was there. It’s still in the acronym, but what do the orgs do for LGB people? Nothing at this point. They are all just TQ+ at this time in history. As I reentered activism, I had to adapt or not get to come back to work or my community, even if I didn’t agree wholesale with everything going on. Given that I had just spent six years in isolation, poor as fuck, and taking the bus everywhere because I had no car, I was faced with a choice around the end of ’22. Enter my TRA era, circa 2023. I did not start off that way as a trans-identified person. For the majority of my transition, I was private and quiet and kept to myself. I wish I could rewind and stop myself right there. I wish I never had a TRA arc. But I did. And I did it for politics and because I had to do what I had to do. Poverty will push you to do what it takes to eat again. It was publicly comply with the leftist queer Marxist agenda or face social and career suicide. LGBT activism morphed into a behemoth of advocacy for TQ+ alone. It happened over a decade. It happened because orgs and NGOs can be very greedy, wanting the money to continue to flow. So much so that we now castrate kids and have giant men in wigs and dresses demanding we call them women. No wonder support has declined.

sample_tweets.author: trynullsec
sample_tweets.text: AI-generated software is becoming its own software supply chain. @cursor_ai, @Lovable, @Replit, @boltdotnew,@claudeai, @grok, agents, dApps, MCP tools, all of them are moving code from prompt to production faster than traditional security workflows can keep up. That creates a new requirement: security validation has to become model-native. Nullsec-S1 is being built for that layer. A security-specialized LLM that sits between AI-generated code and production, analyzing auth logic, secrets, env exposure, SQLi, SSRF, XSS, MCP/tool abuse, shell execution, dependency risk, wallet flows, and safe-code calibration. AI builds the software. Nullsec-S1 validates it.

sample_tweets.text: 🚨CLAUDE’YA VİDEO İZLEME YETENEĞİ: Artık YouTube, TikTok, Loom veya Yerel Videoları Doğrudan İzleyip Analiz Edebiliyor! /watch Komutuyla Gerçek Video Anlama Çağı Başladı! 🎥🔍 Gazetecileri, araştırmacılar, Youtube kanalı işletenler, eğitimciler ve akademisyenler, bu repo sizin hayatınızı kurtaracak... İstediğin videonun analizini yaptır, özetini çıkart, ana fikrini iste... Tam bir isviçre çakısı... bradautomates/claude-video reposu, Claude’un en büyük eksikliklerinden birini tamamen kapatan çok kullanışlı bir açık kaynak skill/plugin. Claude normalde bir YouTube linki aldığında sadece başlığı veya yetersiz transcript’i görebiliyordu. Bu repo ile /watch komutunu kullanarak Claude’a gerçekten video izletiyorsunuz. Nasıl Çalışıyor? ✅URL veya yerel video dosyası veriyorsunuz ✅yt-dlp ile videoyu indiriyor ✅ffmpeg ile akıllıca frame’ler çıkarıyor (süreye göre otomatik ayarlanıyor) ✅Transcript’i alıyor (varsayılan olarak ücretsiz altyazı, yoksa Whisper ile) ✅Tüm frame’leri + timestamp’li transcript’i Claude’a gönderiyor ✅Claude artık videoyu görüyor ve duyuyor, sadece tahmin etmiyor. Kullanım Örnekleri: ✅/watch https://t.co/xs4DREzwjD 30. saniyede ne oluyor? ✅/watch https://t.co/NvnY1WNxjH arayüz nerede bozuluyor? ✅/watch https://t.co/qsGP2P0L4q özetle ✅/watch ~/Videos/egitim.mp4 bu videoda hangi teknikleri öğretiyor? En Çok Kullanılan Senaryolar ✅Viral videoların hook analizleri ✅Reklam ve rakip içerik analizi ✅Bug raporlarını video üzerinden teşhis etme ✅Uzun videoları hızlı özetleme ✅Eğitim ve tutorial videolarını derinlemesine anlama Kurulum Çok Kolay: ✅Claude Code için: /plugin marketplace add bradautomates/claude-video sonra /plugin install watch@claude-video ✅https://t.co/D8Uu99p9iy web için: En son .skill dosyasını indirip Settings → Capabilities → Skills’e sürükle Codex için: Git clone ile skill klasörüne ekle Gerekli araçlar (yt-dlp ve ffmpeg) ilk kullanımda otomatik kuruluyor. Whisper için Groq veya OpenAI API anahtarı opsiyonel. Avantajları: ✅Token tasarrufu için akıllı frame budget (süreye göre otomatik ayarlama) ✅Odak modu (--start ve --end) ile belirli bölümleri yoğun analiz ✅Tamamen yerel çalışabiliyor ✅MIT lisansı ile tamamen özgür Bu repo, Claude’un multimodal yeteneklerini gerçek anlamda kullanışlı hâle getiriyor. Artık video içeriği Claude için de “okunabilir” bir input hâline geldi. Bu skill, özellikle içerik analizi, eğitim, gazetecilik, youtube kanalı, debugging ve araştırma yapan herkes için vazgeçilmez olacak. Sizce Claude’un video izleme yeteneği en çok hangi alanda fark yaratır? Reklam analizi, bug tespiti, video özetleme yoksa başka bir şey mi? Denemek isteyenler “Hemen kuracağım” diye yazsın! Yorumlarda deneyimlerinizi ve favori kullanım senaryolarınızı paylaşın, bu harika Claude skill’ini hep birlikte değerlendirelim! 🎥✨

sample_tweets.author: toshizo2023ip
sample_tweets.text: AIに仕事を相談するとき、つい「全部そのまま貼りたくなる」こと、ありませんか。 クレーム対応、採用の悩み、顧客への返信。具体的であるほど、丸ごと貼りたくなる。 でも、その手前で一歩止まる。私がChatGPTにそのまま投げないデータが、3つあります。 ①個人・顧客が特定できる生データ（実名・連絡先・履歴書・相談ログ） ②未公開の事業・契約・内部情報（価格表・契約書・経営方針） ③認証情報・本番環境（パスワード・APIキー・管理画面ログイン） ※ChatGPTと書きますが、ClaudeやGeminiでも同じ話です。 大事なのは「AIに何も入れるな」ではありません。 実名を伏せる。金額をレンジにする。企業名を業種・規模・地域に言い換える。 情報を捨てるのではなく、粒度を変える。 プロンプトはやり直せても、一度渡した情報は簡単には取り消せない。だからプロンプトを磨く前に、「何を渡し、何を渡さないか」を決めておく。 これが、AIを「使う」段階から「運用する」段階へ進む、最初の関門です。 禁止ではなく、線引きする。怖いから使わない、ではなく、使うために守る。 全10回連載の第4回。 ▼ note記事（全文無料） https://t.co/aHrRcsxsWo

sample_tweets.author: VaibhavSisinty
sample_tweets.text: This guy on Reddit burned 1.15 BILLION Claude tokens in a single month And what he learned will save you thousands of dollars on AI. 🤯 5 takeaways worth saving: → Prompt caching got quietly nerfed: Anthropic cut cache time from 60 minutes to 5, silently raising production costs by 30-60% for most users. → Output tokens cost 5x more than input: Stop asking AI for full text, ask for IDs or numbers and map them in your code — he cut his output bill by 60% doing this. → JSON is a silent token killer: Every bracket, quote, and comma eats tokens, making the same data cost 2x more in JSON than in plain text or markdown tables. → Opus 4.7's tokenizer secretly raised your bill: The new tokenizer generates up to 35% more tokens than Opus 4.6 for the exact same input, and nobody is talking about it. → You're using the wrong model for most tasks: Haiku is 5x cheaper than Opus and good enough for 80% of real work, so stop defaulting to the flagship for everything. The wild part? He runs an AI agent company. This is what he learned by burning real money. While everyone races to use AI, the smart ones are learning how to use it cheaper.

sample_tweets.author: m1shti
sample_tweets.text: I often get asked about the "behind the scenes" history and org structure of @clay's narrative function, so here are some answers! Content + PMM (2023 - 2025): I was a solo IC PMM covering broader messaging and major announcements, and I wrote the three pieces that shaped how people talked about Clay: GTM engineering, GTM alpha, and the GTM IDE. Narrative (mid-2025): Those pieces blew up, concepts started spreading, and we decided to treat the brand story as its own bet. Consolidated socials, influencer, editorial, video, comms, and schemes into one narrative team. (Called it that because I didn't love the term "storytelling.") Education + community formed a separate ecosystem team. Clay Studios (2026): Same scope, rebranded Clay Studios under my hero / bestie Jake Block after I decided to be an IC in-house journalist. Our big push is turning YouTube into a set of video and editorial channels that can fund themselves. At the end of the day, organize it however you want — it depends on the people you have, where they spike, and what they want to do. The name is up to you too, whether that's brand and content, media, narrative, storytelling, studio, etc. All throughout, we've reported into marketing, but that also seems fungible depending on your company. The exact setup matters less than having the function at all. Most companies benefit from telling a good brand story. Media is underfunded, attention still matters, and tech companies with resources are sitting on the ability to stand out and make work that moves people well outside their own bubble. (Also we are def the happiest, laughiest team at the company but we finally managed to mean mug for one picture :) Have added several new family members since then!)

sample_tweets.text: The number should be zero if they understood the queer lifestyle at all. That's why I wrote my book: How do we find the truth without falling into hype or stereotype? One would use the same tools used for any other demographic group. That includes polls, studies, professional opinions, and personal stories. As with polls and studies about categories like gender, nationality, or race, these methods only offer a snapshot of the aggregate. While every individual is unique, the combined picture shows compulsive, miserable, risky people. ...The Warehouse was the nickname for a non-descript business building surrounded by residential homes in northern Minneapolis. Such a discreet location was thought just right for a sex club. Upon entering, patrons were greeted with condoms and water in anticipation of promiscuous sex. Anonymous, voyeuristic and random sex was everywhere on every floor. Open several times a week, the club was entirely ignored by the authorities. No one could be bothered to investigate an unlicensed gay sex club. Single men, husbands, fathers, small-town guys, and bar-hopping men all converged to satisfy their compulsive lusts. Yet this sex club was not in some seedy part of San Francisco before the HIV outbreak, but at a residential part of Minneapolis in 2017. The gay sex house continued its dirty business for a few years until the police finally raided the joint. Oddly enough, someone from the LGBT community had had enough. An older gay man living with AIDS saw a disaster waiting to happen and contacted the police. Yet the raid did not faze the owner, Scott Delage, who moved his club to other locations. “We stopped having parties [there]…But it’s never stopped…” he chuckles. “You know what I mean? There’s a need. There’s an absolute need.” He succinctly described this “need”: “When we started this, word just spread like wildfire…It’s like that old saying, ‘Telephone, telegraph, tell-a-gay.’”

sample_tweets.text: TODAY: Amazon just announced a multi-year plan to invest over €10 billion to expand and modernize its European fulfillment centers, including hiring 25,000 new employees. The investment will include the launch of a next-generation autonomous robot and the expansion of systems that collaborate alongside employees. 🤖🚀 Amazon's autonomous Proteus robot is getting a major upgrade. The original moved heavy carts around dock areas. The new version can work anywhere in our operations, and employees direct it using natural language commands. Also in the mix: Vulcan, Amazon's first robot with a sense of touch, is expanding to handle more tasks at more sites. And STARK, a new collaborative robotic tote-handling system, will be deployed to 15 sites across Europe by 2027.
